342	[Bridge~2: slice-constancy on a saturated open mapping into an affine]
343	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
344	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
345	  % NOTE (iter-161): the iter-160 SIGNATURE GAP is RESOLVED. The Lean proof wires Step 2
346	  % (morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, PROVEN axiom-clean) over Step 1
347	  % (rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, the residual sorry). Step 2 needs the closed
348	  % points of U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson; over k̄ this follows from (X⊗Y) being locally of finite
349	  % type, so [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom] is now a stated hypothesis of this lemma (and across the
350	  % chain). With it, the `JacobsonSpace U` instance is a routine discharge (LocallyOfFiniteType.
351	  % jacobsonSpace + JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding), no longer an as-typed gap. The only `sorry`
352	  % reachable from this lemma is now Step 1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy). The hypothesis
353	  % set is recorded in the statement and proof prose below (both [IsAlgClosed k̄] AND
354	  % [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom]); the earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim
355	  % is retired.
356	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
357	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each $y \in V$, the complete variety $X \times \{y\}$ gets mapped by
358	  % $f$ into the affine variety $U$, and hence to a single point of $U$. But this means that for
359	  % any $x \in X$, $y \in V$, $f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)$, and this proves our
360	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
361	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
362	  ``for each $y \in V$, the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
363	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and let $X$, $Y$, $Z$ be objects of
364	  $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)$ with $X$ proper, $X \times Y$ geometrically irreducible, reduced,
365	  and \emph{locally of finite type} over $\bar k$ (the formalization carries
366	  $[\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]$, which makes the saturated open $U$
367	  below a Jacobson space, so its closed points are dense --- the hypothesis Step~2 of the proof
368	  consumes), and $Z$ separated. Let $f \colon X \times Y \to Z$ be a morphism, fix a $\bar k$-point
369	  $x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X$, and write $\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
370	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)$ for the ``$(x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)$''
371	  endomorphism. Suppose:
372	  \begin{itemize}
373	    \item $U \subseteq X \times Y$ is a $p_2$-\emph{saturated} open, i.e.\ $U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})$
374	      for a set $V_{\mathrm{set}} \subseteq Y$ (so $U$ contains the whole fibre $X_y$ over each of
375	      its points);
376	    \item $U_0 \subseteq Z$ is an \emph{affine} open;
377	    \item $f(U) \subseteq U_0$, i.e.\ $f$ maps $U$ into the affine $U_0$.
378	  \end{itemize}
379	  Then $f$ and $\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f$ agree on $U$ as scheme morphisms:
380	  $U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)$.
381	\end{lemma}
382	
383	\begin{proof}
384	
385	  \uses{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}
386	  This is the formalization realization of Mumford's step ``for each $y \in V$, the complete
387	  variety $X \times \{y\}$ gets mapped by $f$ into the affine variety $U$, and hence to a single
388	  point of $U$''. We realise it by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route that is
389	  \emph{cohomology-free}; in particular the relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
390	  $f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O$ framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no
391	  $H^0$ base change) and is \emph{deliberately avoided}. The argument splits into two named
392	  sub-lemmas, one residual and one proven.
393	
394	  \medskip
395	  \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-closed-slice constancy) --- \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}, the residual.}
396	  Fix a closed point $x$ of $U$, lying over a closed point $y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}$.
397	  Since $\bar k$ is algebraically closed and $Y$ is of finite type over $\bar k$, the residue field
398	  is $\kappa(y) = \bar k$. Because $U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})$ is saturated, $U$ contains the
399	  \emph{entire} fibre $X_y \cong X$ over $y$; hence $f$ maps the proper integral slice $X_y$ into the
400	  affine $U_0$. By $\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}$ the global sections $\Gamma(X_y)$ form a
401	  field, module-finite over $\bar k$ by $\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}$, and
402	  algebraic closedness collapses the finite extension to give $\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k$. A morphism into
403	  the affine $U_0$ is pinned by its ring map on global sections ($\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}$), so the
404	  restriction of $f$ to $X_y$ factors through a single $\bar k$-point of $U_0$ --- necessarily the
405	  point $f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x)$. Thus $f$ and $\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f$
406	  agree (after the residue-field probe) at every closed point of $U$. This per-slice statement is the
407	  chain's single genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
408	
409	  \medskip
410	  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (globalisation over dense closed points) --- \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, proven.}
411	  The closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type $\bar k$-scheme $U$
412	  ($\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}$ / the Jacobson-space property, supplied here by the new hypothesis
413	  $[\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]$). To turn ``$f$ and
414	  $\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f$ agree at each closed point'' into a single morphism equality on $U$,
415	  the proven globalisation \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} assembles the closed points
416	  into one dominant probe --- the coproduct
417	  $\coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,U} \Spec \kappa(x) \to U$, whose range is dense --- and feeds
418	  it to the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity handle $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$ (the same
419	  family $\mathtt{rigidity\_core}$ uses). This yields the morphism equality
420	  $U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)$ on all of $U$. The
421	  ``agrees on a dense set of closed points $\Rightarrow$ hom-equality'' connective is the one piece
422	  Mathlib does not package directly (it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
423	  $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$); that bespoke build is now done in
424	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, and it is \emph{not} cohomology. \qedhere
425	\end{proof}

427	\begin{lemma}\leanok
428	[Step~2: morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a Jacobson source are equal]
429	  \label{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
430	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
431	  % Project-bespoke assembly: NO external source. Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
432	  % ext_of_isDominant; the "dense closed points => hom-extensionality" connective is built here.
433	  Let $W$, $Z$ be schemes with $W$ \emph{reduced}, $W$ a \emph{Jacobson space}, and $Z$
434	  \emph{separated}. Let $g_1, g_2 \colon W \to Z$ be morphisms. If, for every \emph{closed} point
435	  $x \in W$, the two morphisms agree after restriction along the residue-field point
436	  $\Spec \kappa(x) \to W$ (in Lean, $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 =
437	  W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2$), then $g_1 = g_2$.
438	\end{lemma}
439	
440	\begin{proof}
441	  This is the ``dense closed points $\Rightarrow$ hom-extensionality'' connective that Mathlib does
442	  not package directly: it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism extensionality
443	  $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$ (a dominant morphism into a separated target is an epimorphism for
444	  the purpose of right-cancellation). We assemble the per-closed-point hypotheses into one such
445	  dominant probe.
446	
447	  Form the coproduct $P := \coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} \Spec \kappa(x)$ and the morphism
448	  $\mathrm{probe} \colon P \to W$ defined by $\mathtt{Sigma.desc}$ of the residue-field points
449	  $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x$. Its topological range contains every closed point of $W$:
450	  the inclusion $\iota_x \colon \Spec \kappa(x) \to P$ composes with $\mathrm{probe}$ to
451	  $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x$ (the defining property of $\mathtt{Sigma.desc}$), and the
452	  range of $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x$ is the singleton $\{x\}$
453	  ($\mathtt{Scheme.range\_fromSpecResidueField}$). Because $W$ is a Jacobson space, the closed points
454	  are dense ($\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}$, i.e.\ $\overline{\mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} = W$), so
455	  the range of $\mathrm{probe}$ is dense and $\mathrm{probe}$ is \emph{dominant}.
456	
457	  Componentwise, $g_1$ and $g_2$ agree after $\mathrm{probe}$: precomposing with each coproduct
458	  inclusion $\iota_x$ reduces (by $\mathtt{Sigma.\iota\_desc}$) to
459	  $W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 = W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2$,

462	  Since $\mathrm{probe}$ is dominant and $Z$ is separated, $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}$ cancels it

466	\begin{lemma}
467	[Global-sections constancy: two $\bar k$-points of a proper integral source into an affine agree]
468	  \label{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
469	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
470	  % Project-bespoke assembly of Mathlib lemmas: NO external source. Realises, cohomology-free, the
471	  % classical "a global regular function on a proper integral variety over an algebraically closed
472	  % field is constant" --- precisely the affine-image step of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma proof quoted
473	  % above (the "complete slice into an affine maps to a single point" sentence, Ch.~II \S4, p.~43).
474	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and let $W$ be an integral scheme equipped with a structure
475	  morphism $w \colon W \to \Spec \bar k$ that is universally closed (proper) and locally of finite
476	  type. Let $g \colon W \to V$ be a morphism into an \emph{affine} scheme $V$. Then any two
477	  $\bar k$-points of $W$ --- i.e.\ any two sections $a, b \colon \Spec \bar k \to W$ of $w$ (so
478	  $a \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1$ and $b \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1$) --- satisfy $a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g$.
479	
480	  \medskip
481	  \noindent\textbf{Every hypothesis is load-bearing} (confirmed by the iter-161 audit).
482	  $[\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]$ collapses the finite extension $\Gamma(W)/\bar k$; integrality of
483	  $W$ makes $\Gamma(W)$ a domain (without it a two-point disjoint union is a counterexample);
484	  $\mathtt{UniversallyClosed}$ is what upgrades $\Gamma(W)$ to a \emph{field} (without it
485	  $\mathbb A^1$, with $\Gamma = \bar k[t]$ and two distinct $\bar k$-points, is a counterexample);
486	  $\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}$ gives finiteness of $\Gamma(W)$ over $\bar k$; and
487	  $[\mathtt{IsAffine}\ V]$ is what lets $\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}$ pin the morphism by its ring map
488	  on global sections.
489	\end{lemma}
490	
491	\begin{proof}
492	  Because $W$ is integral and $w$ is universally closed, the global sections
493	  $\Gamma(W, \mathcal O_W)$ form a field ($\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}$), and the structure
494	  ring map $w^\sharp \colon \bar k \to \Gamma(W)$ (the map $\mathtt{wk.appTop}$ after the canonical
495	  identification $\Gamma(\Spec \bar k) \cong \bar k$) is integral / module-finite
496	  ($\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}$). Since $\bar k$ is algebraically closed and
497	  $\Gamma(W)$ is a domain finite over it, that map is bijective
498	  ($\mathtt{IsAlgClosed.ringHom\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}$); thus $\Gamma(W) = \bar k$ and
499	  $w^\sharp$ is an isomorphism on global sections --- in particular an epimorphism. Both sections
500	  $a$, $b$ left-invert $w^\sharp$ on global sections (they are sections of $w$), so they induce the
501	  \emph{same} ring map $a^\sharp = b^\sharp$ on $\Gamma(W)$ (cancel the epimorphism $w^\sharp$). A
502	  morphism into the \emph{affine} $V$ is pinned by its global-sections ring map
503	  ($\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}$), whence $a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g$. This realises, without
504	  cohomology, the classical ``proper connected variety into an affine is a single point'' step
505	  quoted from Mumford above.
506	\end{proof}

508	\begin{lemma}
509	[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme]
510	  \label{lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral}
511	  % NOTE: project-bespoke (elementary topology + algebra); NO external source. The \lean{...}
512	  % cross-ref should be filled once the prover lands the named top-level helper this iter (the
513	  % retract-integrality fact `IsIntegral X.left` used in the Step-1 geometric assembly).
514	  Let $X \times Y$ be an integral scheme (here it is geometrically irreducible and reduced over
515	  $\bar k$). Suppose $X$ is a \emph{retract} of $X \times Y$: there is a section
516	  $s \colon X \to X \times Y$ of the first projection $p_1$, i.e.\ $s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X$.
517	  Then $X$ is integral.
518	\end{lemma}
519	
520	\begin{proof}
521	  Integrality is reducedness together with irreducibility, and the retract supplies each.
522	
523	  \emph{Reduced.} The section identity $s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1_X$ makes the pullback on global
524	  sections $p_1^\sharp \colon \mathcal O_X(X) \to \mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)$ split
525	  injective (it is left-inverted by $s^\sharp$). A split-injective ring map into the reduced ring
526	  $\mathcal O_{X \times Y}(X \times Y)$ embeds $\mathcal O_X(X)$ into a reduced ring, so it too is
527	  reduced ($\mathtt{isReduced\_of\_injective}$ on the split injection); hence $X$ is reduced.
528	
529	  \emph{Irreducible.} The first projection $p_1 \colon X \times Y \to X$ is surjective because it
530	  admits the section $s$ (every point of $X$ is $p_1(s(\cdot))$). The continuous image of an
531	  irreducible space under a surjection is irreducible; as $X \times Y$ is irreducible and $p_1$ is a
532	  continuous surjection onto $X$, the space of $X$ is irreducible.
533	
534	  Reduced and irreducible together give that $X$ is integral. This is elementary --- no cohomology,
535	  no external citation --- and feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly below, where the proper slice
536	  $X_y \cong X$ must be presented as proper \emph{integral}.
537	\end{proof}

710	  \textit{Source: Hartshorne, Algebraic Geometry, Example~IV.1.3.5.}
711	  Let $\bar k$ be algebraically closed and $C$ a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
712	  over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
713	\end{proposition}
714	
715	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the verbatim Example IV.1.3.5 text in the statement block above
716	% (Hartshorne states the classification and its proof inside the single Example; the proof is
717	% the second half beginning ``We already know that $p_a(\mathbf P^1) = 0$ \ldots'').
718	\begin{proof}
719	  \uses{def:genus}
720	  \emph{Proof sketch (Riemann--Roch level; the formalisation of this step is itself a
721	  sub-build --- Mathlib has no Riemann--Roch for curves).}
722	
723	  Over the algebraically closed field $\bar k$ every closed point is $\bar k$-rational, so $C$
724	  has at least two distinct $\bar k$-points $P \neq Q$ (a smooth proper geometrically
725	  irreducible curve over $\bar k$ is infinite). With $\genus(C) = \dim_{\bar k}
726	  H^1(C, \mathcal O_C) = 0$, apply the Riemann--Roch theorem to the degree-$0$ divisor
727	  $D = P - Q$. Since $\deg(K - D) = 2g - 2 - \deg D = -2 < 0$ one has $l(K - D) = 0$, so
728	  Riemann--Roch $l(D) - l(K - D) = \deg D + 1 - g$ gives $l(D) = 0 + 1 - 0 = 1$. Thus the
729	  degree-$0$ divisor $D$ is linearly equivalent to an effective divisor, forcing $D \sim 0$,
730	  i.e.\ $P \sim Q$. A curve possessing two distinct linearly equivalent points is rational
731	  (Hartshorne II.6.10.1: $P \sim Q$ for $P \neq Q$ yields a degree-$1$ map to $\mathbb P^1$),
732	  and a complete nonsingular rational curve is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1$ (Hartshorne I.6.12).
733	  Hence $C \cong \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$.
734	\end{proof}
735	
736	\begin{remark}
737	  \label{rmk:genusZero_iso_subbuild}
738	  \cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} is Riemann--Roch--flavoured and its formalisation is a
739	  genuine sub-build, not a short step: Mathlib has no Riemann--Roch theorem for curves, no
740	  divisor class group machinery at this level, and no $\mathbb P^1$-classification of
741	  rational curves. The statement is stated here at textbook level so the chain is complete;
742	  closing its Lean proof will require either importing/porting a substantial slice of curve
743	  theory or re-deriving the genus-$0 \Rightarrow \mathbb P^1$ classification from the
744	  project's own $\genus$ definition. The dependency on $\genus$ (\cref{def:genus}) is the link
745	  to \cref{chap:Genus}.
746	\end{remark}
747	
748	\section{The headline: rigidity for a pointed genus-$0$ curve}
749	
750	\begin{theorem}
751	
752	\leanok
753	[Rigidity: a pointed morphism from a genus-$0$ curve to an abelian variety is constant]
754	  \label{thm:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
755	  \label{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}
756	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme}
757	  \uses{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant, prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
758	  Let $\bar k$ be an algebraically closed field (\emph{arbitrary characteristic}; no
759	  $\mathtt{CharZero}$ hypothesis). Let $C$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
760	  over $\bar k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$, and let $A$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible
761	  group scheme over $\bar k$ (an abelian variety) with identity $\eta_A$. Then for every
762	  morphism $f \colon C \to A$ such that $p \fatsemi f = \eta_A$ for some $\bar k$-point
763	  $p \colon \mathbf 1 \to C$, the morphism $f$ equals the constant morphism at the identity,
764	  $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A$.
765	
766	  This is the project's committed characteristic-free statement that the genus-$0$
767	  Albanese-witness $\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}$ consumes. Its signature mirrors
768	  $\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity\_over\_kbar}$ of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}
769	  \emph{verbatim except} that the $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$ instance is dropped: same
770	  curve typeclasses ($\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 1$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$,
771	  $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$ on $C$), same abelian-variety typeclasses
772	  ($\mathtt{GrpObj}$, $\mathtt{IsProper}$, $\mathtt{Smooth}$, $\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}$
773	  on $A$), and the same pointed-morphism conclusion shape $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi
774	  \eta[A]$.
775	\end{theorem}
776	
777	\begin{proof}
778	  \uses{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant, prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1}
779	  By \cref{prop:genusZero_curve_iso_P1} the genus-$0$ curve $C$ over the algebraically closed
780	  field $\bar k$ is isomorphic to $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ via some isomorphism
781	  $\varphi \colon C \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$. The composite
782	  $f \circ \varphi^{-1} \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to A$ is then a morphism from
783	  $\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}$ to the abelian variety $A$, hence constant by
784	  \cref{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}: its image is a single $\bar k$-point $a_0 \in A$.
785	  Therefore $f$ itself is constant with value $a_0$. The pointed hypothesis
786	  $p \fatsemi f = \eta_A$ evaluates this constant at the $\bar k$-point $p$ and pins it to the
787	  identity, $a_0 = \eta_A$. Consequently $f$ factors through the terminal object as the
788	  constant morphism at the identity, $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \fatsemi \eta_A$.
789	
790	  No differential ($df = 0$) input, no $H^0(C, \Omega_C) = 0$ / Serre-duality decomposition,
791	  and no Picard representability is used; the argument is valid in arbitrary characteristic.
792	  Once formalised it lets the $[\mathtt{CharZero}\ \bar k]$ hypothesis carried by
793	  $\mathtt{rigidity\_over\_kbar}$ be dropped, and (after the $\bar k \to k$ descent step hosted
794	  in \cref{chap:Jacobian}, not here) feeds $\mathtt{genusZeroWitness}.\mathtt{key}$.
795	\end{proof}

variable {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y)

instance (x) : IsLocalHom (f.stalkMap x).hom :=
  f.prop x

@[simp]
lemma stalkMap_id (X : Scheme.{u}) (x : X) :
    (𝟙 X : X ⟶ X).stalkMap x = 𝟙 (X.presheaf.stalk x) :=
  PresheafedSpace.stalkMap.id _ x

lemma stalkMap_comp {X Y Z : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (x : X) :
    (f ≫ g : X ⟶ Z).stalkMap x = g.stalkMap (f x) ≫ f.stalkMap x :=
  PresheafedSpace.stalkMap.comp f.toPshHom g.toPshHom x

@[reassoc]
lemma stalkSpecializes_stalkMap (x x' : X)
    (h : x ⤳ x') : Y.presheaf.stalkSpecializes (f.base.hom.map_specializes h) ≫ f.stalkMap x =
      f.stalkMap x' ≫ X.presheaf.stalkSpecializes h :=

lemma stalkMap_congr (f g : X ⟶ Y) (hfg : f = g) (x x' : X)
    (hxx' : x = x') : f.stalkMap x ≫ (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq hxx')).hom =
      (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq <| hfg ▸ hxx' ▸ rfl)).hom ≫ g.stalkMap x' :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.stalkMap_congr f.toLRSHom g.toLRSHom congr(($hfg).toLRSHom) x x' hxx'

@[reassoc]
lemma stalkMap_congr_hom (f g : X ⟶ Y) (hfg : f = g) (x : X) :
    f.stalkMap x = (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq <| hfg ▸ rfl)).hom ≫ g.stalkMap x :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.stalkMap_congr_hom f.toLRSHom g.toLRSHom congr(($hfg).toLRSHom) x

@[reassoc]
lemma stalkMap_congr_point (x x' : X) (hxx' : x = x') :
    f.stalkMap x ≫ (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq hxx')).hom =
      (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq <| hxx' ▸ rfl)).hom ≫ f.stalkMap x' :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.stalkMap_congr_point f.toLRSHom x x' hxx'

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma stalkMap_hom_inv (e : X ≅ Y) (y : Y) :
    e.hom.stalkMap (e.inv y) ≫ e.inv.stalkMap y =
      (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq (by simp))).hom :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.stalkMap_hom_inv (forgetToLocallyRingedSpace.mapIso e) y

@[simp]
lemma stalkMap_hom_inv_apply (e : X ≅ Y) (y : Y) (z) :
    e.inv.stalkMap y (e.hom.stalkMap (e.inv y) z) =
      (Y.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq (by simp))).hom z :=
  DFunLike.congr_fun (CommRingCat.hom_ext_iff.mp (stalkMap_hom_inv e y)) z

@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
lemma stalkMap_inv_hom (e : X ≅ Y) (x : X) :
    e.inv.stalkMap (e.hom x) ≫ e.hom.stalkMap x =
      (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq (by simp))).hom :=
  LocallyRingedSpace.stalkMap_inv_hom (forgetToLocallyRingedSpace.mapIso e) x

@[simp]
lemma stalkMap_inv_hom_apply (e : X ≅ Y) (x : X) (y) :
    e.hom.stalkMap x (e.inv.stalkMap (e.hom x) y) =
      (X.presheaf.stalkCongr (.of_eq (by simp))).hom y :=
  DFunLike.congr_fun (CommRingCat.hom_ext_iff.mp (stalkMap_inv_hom e x)) y

lemma germ_stalkMap (U : Y.Opens) (x : X) (hx : f x ∈ U) :

---usage---
theorem isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion {X Y : Scheme} (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsReduced Y] : IsReduced X := by
  constructor
  intro U
  rw [← f.preimage_image_eq U]
  exact isReduced_of_injective (inv <| f.app (f ''ᵁ U)).hom
    (asIso <| f.app (f ''ᵁ U) : Γ(Y, f ''ᵁ U) ≅ _).symm.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective

instance {X : Scheme} {U : X.Opens} [IsReduced X] : IsReduced U :=
    isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion U.ι

instance {R : CommRingCat.{u}} [H : _root_.IsReduced R] : IsReduced (Spec R) := by
  apply +allowSynthFailures isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk
  intro x; dsimp
  have : _root_.IsReduced (CommRingCat.of <| Localization.AtPrime (PrimeSpectrum.asIdeal x)) := by
    dsimp; infer_instance
  exact isReduced_of_injective (Spec.stalkIso R x).hom.hom
    (Spec.stalkIso R x).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv.injective

theorem isReduced_of_injective [MonoidWithZero R] [MonoidWithZero S] {F : Type*}
    [FunLike F R S] [MonoidWithZeroHomClass F R S]
    (f : F) (hf : Function.Injective f) [IsReduced S] :
    IsReduced R := by
  constructor
  intro x hx
  apply hf
  rw [map_zero]
  exact (hx.map f).eq_zero

instance (ι) (R : ι → Type*) [∀ i, Zero (R i)] [∀ i, Pow (R i) ℕ]
    [∀ i, IsReduced (R i)] : IsReduced (∀ i, R i) where
  eq_zero _ := fun ⟨n, hn⟩ ↦ funext fun i ↦ IsReduced.eq_zero _ ⟨n, congr_fun hn i⟩

/-- **Integrality descends to a retract (blueprint `lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral`).**
If `T` is an integral scheme and `S` is a *retract* of `T` — i.e. there are `r : S ⟶ T` and
`pr : T ⟶ S` with `r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S` — then `S` is integral.

Two halves, both elementary (no cohomology):
* *Irreducible.* `pr` is a continuous surjection (it has the section `r`, so `pr.base ∘ r.base =
  id`), and the continuous surjective image of the irreducible `T` is irreducible.
* *Reduced.* For each `x : S`, the stalk map `pr.stalkMap (r x) : 𝒪_{S,x} ⟶ 𝒪_{T, r x}` is split
  injective: composing with `r.stalkMap x` gives `(r ≫ pr).stalkMap x`, an isomorphism (since
  `r ≫ pr = 𝟙`). Hence `𝒪_{S,x}` embeds into the reduced stalk `𝒪_{T, r x}`
  (`isReduced_of_injective`), so every stalk of `S` is reduced (`isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`).

Reduced and irreducible together give `IsIntegral S`
(`isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`). This feeds the Step-1 geometric assembly, where
the proper slice `X_y ≅ X` must be presented as proper *integral*. -/
theorem isIntegral_of_retract {S T : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral T]
    (r : S ⟶ T) (pr : T ⟶ S) (hrp : r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S) : IsIntegral S := by
  -- `pr.base` is surjective: `r.base` is a section of it.
  have hsurj : Function.Surjective pr.base := by
    intro x
    refine ⟨r.base x, ?_⟩
    have h := congrArg (fun m => m.base x) hrp
    simpa using h
  -- Irreducibility: continuous surjective image of the irreducible `T`.
  haveI : IrreducibleSpace S := by
    rw [irreducibleSpace_def]
    have h := (IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ T).image pr.base
      pr.base.hom.continuous.continuousOn
    rwa [Set.image_univ, hsurj.range_eq] at h
  -- Reducedness: each stalk of `S` embeds into the corresponding reduced stalk of `T`.
  haveI : IsReduced S := by
    apply isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk
    intro x
    -- `(r ≫ pr).stalkMap x` is an isomorphism (`r ≫ pr = 𝟙`), equal to
    -- `pr.stalkMap (r x) ≫ r.stalkMap x`, so the first factor is injective.
    haveI hiso : IsIso ((r ≫ pr).stalkMap x) := by
      rw [stalkMap_congr_hom (r ≫ pr) (𝟙 S) hrp x, stalkMap_id, Category.comp_id]
      infer_instance
    rw [stalkMap_comp] at hiso
    have hbij := (ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective
      (pr.stalkMap (r.base x) ≫ r.stalkMap x)).1 hiso
    have hinj : Function.Injective (pr.stalkMap (r.base x)).hom := by
      intro a b hab
      apply hbij.injective
      rw [ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
      exact congrArg (r.stalkMap x) hab
    have hb : pr.base (r.base x) = x := by
      have h := congrArg (fun m => m.base x) hrp
      simpa using h
    have hred : IsReduced (S.presheaf.stalk (pr.base (r.base x))) :=
      isReduced_of_injective (pr.stalkMap (r.base x)).hom hinj
    rwa [hb] at hred
  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced S

/-- **Per-closed-slice constancy (Step 1 of bridge 2's route B), the residual deep geometry.**

-- corestricts `(s ≫ f).left : X.left → Z.left` to `U₀.toScheme` and applies the sub-lemma to the
  -- two `k̄`-points (the `X`-coordinate of `q`, and of its `X`-collapse) of the proper integral `X`.
  -- Name the collapse endomorphism `retract := (x, y) ↦ (x₀, y)`.
  set retract : X ⊗ Y ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (toUnit (X ⊗ Y) ≫ x₀) (snd X Y) with hretract
  -- `(toUnit X).left = X.hom`.
  have htoUnit : (toUnit X).left = X.hom := by simp
  -- The `Y`-coordinate of `q` is a `k̄`-point `ŷ : 𝟙_ ⟶ Y` (a section of `Y.hom`).
  have hysec : (q ≫ (snd X Y).left) ≫ Y.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [Category.assoc, Over.w, hqsec]
  set yhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ Y :=
    Over.homMk (q ≫ (snd X Y).left) hysec with hyhat
  have hyhatL : yhat.left = q ≫ (snd X Y).left := rfl
  -- The slice section `sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, ŷ)`, and its underlying scheme map `sL`.
  set sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ yhat) with hsecdef
  set sL : X.left ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left := sec.left with hsLdef
  have hsLfst : sL ≫ (fst X Y).left = 𝟙 X.left := by
    rw [hsLdef, ← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
  have hsLsnd : sL ≫ (snd X Y).left = X.hom ≫ yhat.left := by
    rw [hsLdef, ← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_snd, Over.comp_left, htoUnit]
  -- `retract` fixes the `Y`-coordinate.
  have hretsnd : retract.left ≫ (snd X Y).left = (snd X Y).left := by
    rw [← Over.comp_left, hretract, lift_snd]
  -- The two `k̄`-points of `X.left`: `X`-coordinates of `q` and of the collapsed point.
  set aL : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ X.left := q ≫ (fst X Y).left with haLdef
  set bL : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ X.left := q ≫ retract.left ≫ (fst X Y).left with hbLdef
  -- Both are sections of the structure map `X.hom`.
  have haLsec : aL ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [haLdef, Category.assoc, Over.w (fst X Y), hqsec]
  have hbLsec : bL ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [hbLdef]; simp only [Category.assoc]
    rw [Over.w (fst X Y), Over.w retract, hqsec]
  -- Pullback identities: `q` and the collapsed point are images of `aL`, `bL` under the slice
  -- section `sL` (`X.left ≅ X_y ⊆ X ⊗ Y`).
  have hI : q = aL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left, Category.assoc aL sL, hsLfst, Category.comp_id]
      exact haLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left, Category.assoc aL sL, hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc aL X.hom, haLsec,
        Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
  have hII : q ≫ retract.left = bL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left, Category.assoc bL sL, hsLfst, Category.comp_id,
        Category.assoc q retract.left]
      exact hbLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left, Category.assoc bL sL, hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc bL X.hom, hbLsec,
        Category.id_comp, hyhatL, Category.assoc q retract.left, hretsnd]
  -- `IsIntegral X.left`: `X.left` is a retract of the integral product via the section `sL`.
  haveI : IsIntegral (X ⊗ Y).left := by
    haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
      GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
    exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
  haveI : IsIntegral X.left := isIntegral_of_retract sL (fst X Y).left hsLfst
  -- `sec ≫ f` maps the slice into the affine `U₀`; corestrict to `U₀.toScheme`.
  haveI : IsAffine U₀.toScheme := _hU₀
  have hrange : Set.range ((sec ≫ f).left).base ⊆ Set.range U₀.ι.base := by
    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]

The root cause: `set`-bound `aL`/`bL` get zeta-unfolded in `pullback.hom_ext` subgoals, so `Category.assoc` grabs the wrong composite. Fix: `clear_value aL bL` to make them opaque, then the assoc rewrites target correctly.

have hI : q = aL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact haLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, haLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
  have hII : q ≫ retract.left = bL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact hbLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hretsnd, hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, hbLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
input.new_string:
-- Make `aL`, `bL` opaque so `Category.assoc` targets the outer composite (not their bodies).
  clear_value aL bL
  have hI : q = aL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact haLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, haLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
  have hII : q ≫ retract.left = bL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact hbLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hretsnd, hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, hbLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]

    · rw [← Over.fst_left];

-- The `Y`-coordinate of `q` is a `k̄`-point `ŷ : 𝟙_ ⟶ Y` (a section of `Y.hom`).
  have hysec : (q ≫ (snd X Y).left) ≫ Y.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [Category.assoc, Over.w, hqsec]
  set yhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ Y :=
    Over.homMk (q ≫ (snd X Y).left) hysec with hyhat
  have hyhatL : yhat.left = q ≫ (snd X Y).left := rfl
  -- The slice section `sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, ŷ)`, and its underlying scheme map `sL`.
  set sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ yhat) with hsecdef
  set sL : X.left ⟶ (X ⊗ Y).left := sec.left with hsLdef
  have hsLfst : sL ≫ (fst X Y).left = 𝟙 X.left := by
    rw [hsLdef, ← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
  have hsLsnd : sL ≫ (snd X Y).left = X.hom ≫ yhat.left := by
    rw [hsLdef, ← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_snd]; simp [htoUnit]
  -- `retract` fixes the `Y`-coordinate.
  have hretsnd : retract.left ≫ (snd X Y).left = (snd X Y).left := by
    rw [← Over.comp_left, hretract, lift_snd]
  -- The two `k̄`-points of `X.left`: `X`-coordinates of `q` and of the collapsed point.
  set aL : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ X.left := q ≫ (fst X Y).left with haLdef
  set bL : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶ X.left := q ≫ retract.left ≫ (fst X Y).left with hbLdef
  -- Both are sections of the structure map `X.hom`.
  have haLsec : aL ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [haLdef, Category.assoc, Over.w (fst X Y), hqsec]
  have hbLsec : bL ≫ X.hom = 𝟙 (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)) := by
    rw [hbLdef]; simp only [Category.assoc]
    rw [Over.w (fst X Y), Over.w retract, hqsec]
  -- Pullback identities: `q` and the collapsed point are images of `aL`, `bL` under the slice
  -- section `sL` (`X.left ≅ X_y ⊆ X ⊗ Y`).
  -- Make `aL`, `bL` opaque so `Category.assoc` targets the outer composite (not their bodies).
  clear_value aL bL
  have hI : q = aL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact haLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, haLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
  have hII : q ≫ retract.left = bL ≫ sL := by
    apply pullback.hom_ext
    · rw [← Over.fst_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hsLfst, Category.comp_id]; exact hbLdef.symm
    · rw [← Over.snd_left]; simp only [Category.assoc]
      rw [hretsnd, hsLsnd, ← Category.assoc, hbLsec, Category.id_comp, hyhatL]
  -- `IsIntegral X.left`: `X.left` is a retract of the integral product via the section `sL`.
  haveI : IsIntegral (X ⊗ Y).left := by
    haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
      GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
    exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
  haveI : IsIntegral X.left := isIntegral_of_retract sL (fst X Y).left hsLfst
  -- `sec ≫ f` maps the slice into the affine `U₀`; corestrict to `U₀.toScheme`.
  haveI : IsAffine U₀.toScheme := _hU₀
  have hrange : Set.range ((sec ≫ f).left).base ⊆ Set.range U₀.ι.base := by
    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
    rintro _ ⟨t, rfl⟩
    have hmemU : sL.base t ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
      rw [_hUV, Set.mem_preimage]
      have e1 : (snd X Y).left.base (sL.base t)
          = (snd X Y).left.base (q.base (X.hom.base t)) := by
        have h2 := congrArg (fun m : X.left ⟶ Y.left => m.base t) hsLsnd
        simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply] at h2
        rw [h2, hyhatL, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
      rw [e1]
      have hqmem : q.base (X.hom.base t) ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
        rw [hq, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, pointOfClosedPoint_apply, ← Scheme.Opens.range_ι]

    rw [Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← hsLdef]

  have hgfac : g ≫ U₀.ι = (sec ≫ f).left := IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange

  have key : aL ≫ g = bL ≫ g :=

  have hsLf : sL ≫ f.left = (sec ≫ f).left := by rw [hsLdef, ← Over.comp_left]

  have hgoal : q ≫ f.left = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left := by

      _ = (aL ≫ g) ≫ U₀.ι := by rw [Category.assoc]
      _ = (bL ≫ g) ≫ U₀.ι := by rw [key]
      _ = bL ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by rw [Category.assoc, hgfac]
      _ = (bL ≫ sL) ≫ f.left := by rw [← hsLf, ← Category.assoc]
      _ = (q ≫ retract.left) ≫ f.left := by rw [← hII]
      _ = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left := by rw [Category.assoc]

    _ = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left := hgoal
    _ = px ≫ U.ι ≫ Over.Hom.left retract ≫ Over.Hom.left f := by rw [hq, Category.assoc]

-- Lift `q` to a genuine `k̄`-point `q̂ : 𝟙_ ⟶ X ⊗ Y` of the product (a section of `(X⊗Y).hom`),
  -- working in `Over (Spec k̄)` where the cartesian-monoidal algebra is clean.
  set qhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ X ⊗ Y := Over.homMk q hqsec with hqhat
  have hqhatL : qhat.left = q := rfl
  -- Its `X`- and `Y`-coordinates, and the slice section `sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y`, `x ↦ (x, ŷ)`.
  set yhat : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ Y := qhat ≫ snd X Y with hyhat
  set xq : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar))) ⟶ X := qhat ≫ fst X Y with hxq
  set sec : X ⟶ X ⊗ Y := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ yhat) with hsecdef
  clear_value qhat xq yhat
  -- Slice identities in `Over (Spec k̄)`: `q̂ = xq ≫ sec` and `q̂ ≫ retract = x₀ ≫ sec`.
  have hIover : qhat = xq ≫ sec := by
    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_fst, Category.comp_id]; exact hxq.symm
    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_snd, ← Category.assoc,
        toUnit_unique (xq ≫ toUnit X) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]; exact hyhat.symm
  have hIIover : qhat ≫ retract = x₀ ≫ sec := by
    apply CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext
    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_fst, Category.comp_id, hretract, Category.assoc, lift_fst,
        ← Category.assoc, toUnit_unique (qhat ≫ toUnit (X ⊗ Y)) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp]
    · rw [Category.assoc, hsecdef, lift_snd, ← Category.assoc,
        toUnit_unique (x₀ ≫ toUnit X) (𝟙 _), Category.id_comp, hyhat, hretract, Category.assoc,
        lift_snd]
  -- `sec.left` is a section of the first projection, exhibiting `X.left` as a retract.
  have hsecLfst : sec.left ≫ (fst X Y).left = 𝟙 X.left := by
    rw [← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_fst, Over.id_left]
  have hsecLsnd : sec.left ≫ (snd X Y).left = X.hom ≫ yhat.left := by
    rw [← Over.comp_left, hsecdef, lift_snd]; simp [htoUnit]
  -- `IsIntegral X.left`: `X.left` is a retract of the integral product via the section `sec.left`.
  haveI : IsIntegral (X ⊗ Y).left := by
    haveI : IrreducibleSpace (X ⊗ Y).left :=
      GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton (X ⊗ Y).hom
    exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced _
  haveI : IsIntegral X.left := isIntegral_of_retract sec.left (fst X Y).left hsecLfst
  -- `sec ≫ f` maps the slice into the affine `U₀`; corestrict to `U₀.toScheme`.
  haveI : IsAffine U₀.toScheme := _hU₀
  -- The slice `sec.left` lands in the saturated open `U` (the fibre over `ŷ ∈ Vset`).
  have hsecU : ∀ t : X.left, sec.left.base t ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
    intro t
    rw [_hUV, Set.mem_preimage]
    have e1 : (snd X Y).left.base (sec.left.base t)
        = (snd X Y).left.base (q.base (X.hom.base t)) := by
      have h2 := congrArg (fun m : X.left ⟶ Y.left => m.base t) hsecLsnd
      simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_apply] at h2
      rw [h2, hyhat]
      simp only [Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, hqhatL]
    rw [e1]
    have hqmem : q.base (X.hom.base t) ∈ (↑U : Set (X ⊗ Y).left) := by
      rw [hq, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, pointOfClosedPoint_apply, ← Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
      exact Set.mem_range_self x
    rw [_hUV, Set.mem_preimage] at hqmem
    exact hqmem
  have hrange : Set.range ((sec ≫ f).left).base ⊆ Set.range U₀.ι.base := by
    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
    rintro _ ⟨t, rfl⟩
    have hfin := _hfU (sec.left.base t) (hsecU t)
    rw [Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
    exact hfin
  set g : X.left ⟶ U₀.toScheme := IsOpenImmersion.lift U₀.ι (sec ≫ f).left hrange with hgdef
  have hgfac : g ≫ U₀.ι = (sec ≫ f).left := IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ hrange
  -- The deep algebra: two `k̄`-points (`xq`, `x₀`) of the proper integral slice into the affine agree.
  have key : xq.left ≫ g = x₀.left ≫ g :=
    eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral X.hom g xq.left x₀.left (Over.w xq) (Over.w x₀)
  -- Translate the Over-level slice identities to the `q`-form of the goal.
  have hxqf : q ≫ f.left = xq.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by
    rw [← hqhatL, ← Over.comp_left, hIover, Category.assoc, Over.comp_left]
  have hx₀f : q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left = x₀.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by
    rw [← Over.comp_left, ← hqhatL, ← Over.comp_left, ← Category.assoc, hIIover, Category.assoc,
      Over.comp_left]
  calc px ≫ U.ι ≫ Over.Hom.left f
      = q ≫ f.left := by rw [hq, Category.assoc]
    _ = xq.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := hxqf
    _ = xq.left ≫ g ≫ U₀.ι := by rw [hgfac]
    _ = (xq.left ≫ g) ≫ U₀.ι := by rw [Category.assoc]
    _ = (x₀.left ≫ g) ≫ U₀.ι := by rw [key]
    _ = x₀.left ≫ (sec ≫ f).left := by rw [Category.assoc, hgfac]
    _ = q ≫ retract.left ≫ f.left := hx₀f.symm
    _ = px ≫ U.ι ≫ Over.Hom.left retract ≫ Over.Hom.left f := by rw [hq, Category.assoc]

# AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean

## SUMMARY (iter-162)

**RIGIDITY-LEMMA CHAIN FULLY CLOSED, AXIOM-CLEAN.** The lone deep residual `sorry`
(`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, Step 1) is now PROVEN. `lean_verify` on
`rigidity_lemma`, `rigidity_core` (transitively), `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`,
`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, and the new helper `isIntegral_of_retract` all
return ONLY `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — no `sorryAx`.

File status: `lean_diagnostic_messages` → `success: true`. Remaining 3 sorries are the
OFF-LIMITS deferred scaffolds only (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const` L795,
`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` L819, `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` L844) — untouched per PROGRESS.

## isIntegral_of_retract (NEW named top-level helper, L186-ish)
### Attempt 1 — RESOLVED, axiom-clean
- **Statement:** `{S T : Scheme} [IsIntegral T] (r : S ⟶ T) (pr : T ⟶ S) (hrp : r ≫ pr = 𝟙 S) : IsIntegral S`.
  Blueprint node `lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral`. (Phrased generically about schemes, not
  the Over category — cleaner and reusable.)
- **Irreducible:** `pr.base` surjective (section `r.base`), so `IsIrreducible.image` of
  `IrreducibleSpace.isIrreducible_univ T` + `hsurj.range_eq` ⟹ `irreducibleSpace_def`.
- **Reduced:** per-stalk. `(r ≫ pr).stalkMap x` is an iso (`stalkMap_congr_hom … 𝟙`, `stalkMap_id`,
  the residual `IsIso (stalkCongr.hom ≫ 𝟙)` discharged by **`exact inferInstanceAs (IsIso (…≫ 𝟙 _))`**
  — bare `infer_instance`/`Category.comp_id` both FAILED here, the `inferInstanceAs` shape-hint is the
  fix). `stalkMap_comp` ⟹ `pr.stalkMap (r x)` injective (via `ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective` +
  `Function.Injective`). `isReduced_of_injective` into reduced `T.stalk`; `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk`.
  Then `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
- **KEY pin:** `isReduced_of_isReduced_stalk` takes the stalk-reducedness as an **instance arg**
  (`[∀ x, IsReduced (stalk x)]`), NOT via `apply … ; intro x`. Build `haveI hstalk : ∀ x, …` first.

## rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine (L204 decl, body was sorry L317) — RESOLVED
### Winning approach: Over-level cartesian algebra, NOT scheme-level pullback.hom_ext
- **Dead end (do NOT repeat):** proving the slice identities `q = aL ≫ sL` via `pullback.hom_ext`
  on `(X⊗Y).left`. `Category.assoc` / `simp [Category.assoc]` **cannot match** `(aL ≫ sL) ≫ pullback.fst`
  because the composite's middle object is syntactically `(X⊗Y).left` while `pullback.fst`'s domain is
  `pullback X.hom Y.hom` (defeq but keyed-matching fails). `clear_value` did not help.
- **Fix:** lift `q` to `qhat := Over.homMk q hqsec : 𝟙_ ⟶ X⊗Y` (works because `(𝟙_).left` is defeq
  `Spec k̄` and `(𝟙_).hom` defeq `𝟙`). Do ALL slice algebra in `Over (Spec k̄)`:
  `xq := qhat ≫ fst`, `yhat := qhat ≫ snd`, `sec := lift (𝟙 X) (toUnit X ≫ yhat)`.
  Prove `hIover : qhat = xq ≫ sec` and `hIIover : qhat ≫ retract = x₀ ≫ sec` by
  **`CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext`** (check after `fst`/`snd`) + `lift_fst`/`lift_snd` +
  **`toUnit_unique (… ≫ toUnit _) (𝟙 _)`** for the `_ ≫ toUnit = 𝟙` collapse. These are clean (no
  pullback-object mismatch).
- **Slice→affine:** `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral X.hom g xq.left x₀.left (Over.w xq) (Over.w x₀)`
  with `g := IsOpenImmersion.lift U₀.ι (sec≫f).left hrange`, `IsAffine U₀.toScheme := _hU₀`,
  `IsIntegral X.left := isIntegral_of_retract sec.left (fst X Y).left hsecLfst`. `Over.w xq/x₀` supply the
  section hyps (`(𝟙_).hom` defeq `𝟙` makes them typecheck against `… = 𝟙 (Spec k̄)`). `[UniversallyClosed
  X.hom]`/`[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]` are FREE from `[IsProper X.hom]` (IsProper extends both).
- **Range bound (`hsecU`):** `sec.left` lands in `U` because `(snd).left ∘ sec.left = X.hom ≫ q ≫ (snd).left`
  (hsecLsnd) and `q.base _ = U.ι.base x ∈ ↑U` (via `pointOfClosedPoint_apply` + `Scheme.Opens.range_ι`),
  `U = p₂⁻¹ Vset` saturated.
- **Assembly to goal:** `hxqf`/`hx₀f` via `simpa only [Over.comp_left, hqhatL] using congrArg Over.Hom.left h…`;
  bridge `xq.left ≫ (sec≫f).left = x₀.left ≫ (sec≫f).left` via `key` + `hgfac`; chain by `.trans`;
  fold `px ≫ U.ι → q` with `rw [hq] at hgoalq; simpa only [Category.assoc]`.

### Tooling traps recorded
- `rw [… , last_lemma]` sometimes leaves a SYNTACTICALLY-refl goal UNSOLVED (e.g. hyhatL). Workaround:
  end with `exact congrArg (· ≫ g) h` instead of relying on rw's auto-rfl.
- The big `calc` failed `Trans Eq Eq ?m` synthesis (cause unclear); replaced by explicit `.trans` chain.

## Blueprint markers ready (sync_leanok will set \leanok)
- `lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral` — now has a Lean witness `AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract`.
  **Plan/review: add `\lean{AlgebraicGeometry.isIntegral_of_retract}` to that node** (its `\lean{}` was
  left blank pending this helper).
- `lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, `lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`,
  `lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open`, `thm:rigidity_lemma`, `rigidity_core` — all now `sorry`-free + axiom-clean
  (the whole chain delaunders).

## Next
- Chain is DONE. iter-163 base-case route decision (cube vs hybrid vs Pic⁰) is unblocked and decoupled.

last]` sometimes leaves a syntactically-refl goal UNSOLVED — end with `exact congrArg (·≫g) h` instead.

The lone genuinely-deep residual `sorry` (`rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine`, Step 1 of bridge 2) is now **fully proven**. `lean_verify` confirms `rigidity_lemma`, `rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine`, the Step-1 lemma, and the new helper all carry only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — **no `sorryAx`**. The file compiles (`success: true`); the only remaining sorries are the 3 explicitly off-limits deferred scaffolds (`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`, `genusZero_curve_iso_P1`, `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme`).

**What I built:**
1. **`isIntegral_of_retract`** (new named top-level helper, axiom-clean) — integrality descends to a retract: irreducibility via continuous-surjective image; reducedness per-stalk via the split-injective stalk map. This is the keystone the progress-critic tripwire required, and the blueprint node `lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral`.
2. **The full Step-1 geometric assembly** — the section `ŷ`/`sec`, integrality of the slice, corestriction via `IsOpenImmersion.lift`, and the two-point identities feeding the proven `eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral`.

**Key technical pivot:** the planned `pullback.hom_ext` route is unworkable (`Category.assoc` fails to keyed-match because `(X⊗Y).left` and `pullback X.hom Y.hom` differ syntactically). I lifted the `k̄`-point `q` to `Over (Spec k̄)` via `Over.homMk` and did all slice algebra there with `CartesianMonoidalCategory.hom_ext` + `lift_fst/snd` + `toUnit_unique`, then descended via `.left`. Documented this and several tooling traps in the task result and memory.

This closes a foundational, route-independent asset (it also feeds Route A's Albanese UP), so it stands regardless of the iter-163 base-case route decision.
